title: MedievalMC Client
description: A modpack/client constructed to revamp the vanilla experience to give better performance with increased functionality.
body:
A "**Better Vanilla**" Modpack/Client that aims to **improve** and **expand** on the base game. it does so by adding new features such as free-cam, **shader support**  and   more. The modpack also adds many **QOL features** such as tweaks to GUI, mouse actions, and **full-bright**.


<details>
<summary>Full List Of Additions</summary>

more animations, **improved preformance**, in-game skin swaping, physics, in game resourcepack shaderpack and vanilla-tweaks downloading, fog control, custom main menu, CIT support, optifine entity support, configurable zoom, quicker launch times, ingame music view for services such as spotify, auto equiping totems, auto elytra equiping and unequiping, sneaking and opening a menu now keeps you crouched, better looking skins  using 3D skin layers, ingame cosmetics using the Essentials Mod, multiplayer world hosting, fully reworked 3rd person camera, Jade mod to identify blocks and see entity data, eating animations, improved durabilty tooltip, entity culling, better book writing, better looking explosions, wakes behind swimming entitys and boats, item-locks to prevent accidental item drops, visible rarity levels on items in GUI, dynamic lights, better mouse behavior, better advancement pop-ups, CIT browser for item names to make the CIT experience better, armor transparency controls to make the multiplayer expirence better, Simple Voice Chat for servers that support it and all localy hosted worlds, timer bars on all active potion effects, visuality mod for more particles, footstep sound overhaul, added ambience such as birds and other animal noises in forest, sound physics to make all sounds feel more concrete and positional, mini-map world-map and waypoint support for keeping track of all of your in game locations, 3D piston necks, crashes now send you to the main menu instead of completely closing the game, notification when game is done loading, and many more additions that are less important and do not need mentioning.

</details>
